resources.string: Paste one URL/ID, or many separated by newline / space / comma / semicolon
Full DPI bypass through your deployed Apps Script relay.

No relay. Google + configured fronting groups get DPI bypass; everything else is raw TCP (instant, no overhead). Pick this if you only need Google access, or as a bootstrap to open script.google.com and deploy Code.gs.
All traffic tunneled end-to-end through Apps Script + remote tunnel node. No certificate needed.

Local DPI bypass for every TLS host (not just Google). The real ClientHello is split across TCP segments and sent direct to the destination — no Apps Script, no VPS, no MITM cert. Pick this for full DPI coverage; costs ~300 ms per TLS handshake vs. raw TCP. Cannot bypass IP-level blocks (claude.ai, x.ai, …).

Every TCP session is encrypted and uploaded to a Google Drive folder you own; a separate rahgozar-drive-relay binary on a VPS abroad polls the folder and forwards. ISP only sees TLS to *.google.com. Requires a VPS + relay binary + Drive folder + relay public key. Slower than Apps Script (no long-poll on Drive).
Connect to Google Drive (one-time), pick a folder for the encrypted mailbox, paste the public key your relay printed at `rahgozar-drive-relay keygen` time.

Register your own TVs and Limited Input devices OAuth client in Google Cloud Console — see docs/drive_oauth_setup.md for the walkthrough. Required: every user supplies their own client so the 100-user cap on unverified clients never bites.
Paste your OAuth client_id and client_secret above and Save before signing in.
Bare Drive folder ID (the random string in the URL after /folders/). Both this client and the relay must use the same folder.
Bech32m public key your relay printed (starts with `rgdr1`). The checksum catches typos.
Couldn\'t open browser. Copy this URL: %1$s
Open %1$s in any browser and enter the code below. The app will pick up the sign-in automatically.
Waiting for sign-in… Leave this dialog open.
Sign-in code expired. Tap Sign in again.
OAuth start returned an incomplete response.
Config too large for QR code
Importing routes your traffic through the deployment IDs in this config. Only import from trusted sources.

rahgozar creates a local certificate authority so it can decrypt and re-encrypt HTTPS traffic before tunnelling it through the Apps Script relay. Without this CA installed as trusted, apps will show certificate errors.

On Android 11+ the system removed the inline install path, so tapping Install will: (1) save a PEM copy to Downloads/rahgozar-ca.crt, (2) open the Settings app.\n\nInside Settings, tap the search bar and type \"CA certificate\". Open the result labelled \"CA certificate\" (NOT \"VPN & app user certificate\" or \"Wi-Fi certificate\"). Pick rahgozar-ca.crt from Downloads when prompted. If you don\'t have a screen lock, Android will ask you to add one first — that\'s an OS requirement for installing any user CA.
Could not read the CA cert yet. Tap Start once so the proxy generates it, then come back.
Certificate not yet installed. Saved to %1$s. In Settings, search for \"CA certificate\" and install from there — NOT \"VPN & app user certificate\" or \"Wi-Fi\".
Certificate not yet installed. Tap Install again to retry.

Full URLs (https://script.google.com/macros/s/.../exec) or bare IDs. Paste many at once into the Add field — they\'ll be split on newline / space / comma / semicolon. Multiple IDs are rotated round-robin — more IDs = more pipeline throughput in full mode.
The shared secret you set in the Apps Script.
Requests the OS VPN grant on Connect. All device traffic is routed automatically.
No OS VPN. Set your Wi-Fi proxy to 127.0.0.1:%1$d (HTTP) or %2$d (SOCKS5) after Connect. Only apps that honour the proxy settings will tunnel.
Enabled SNIs are rotated when connecting to google_ip. Leaving all unchecked lets Rust auto-expand the default Google pool.
Choose which apps go through the VPN. Only applies in VPN (TUN) mode. Our own app is always excluded to avoid routing its own traffic back through the tunnel.
Off disables cert checks for the Google edge. Only useful for debugging.
Route youtube.com / youtu.be / ytimg.com through Apps Script relay instead of SNI-rewrite tunnel. Avoids restricted mode but slower for video.
Fan-out per request. 1 is normal; bump to 2-3 on lossy links.
Drop UDP/443 so browsers use TCP/HTTPS. QUIC over TCP tunnel causes meltdown.
Reject browser DoH — forces instant system DNS via tun2proxy. Saves ~1.5s per domain lookup.
Send browser DoH direct, not through tunnel. Faster DNS — queries are still encrypted.
If set, route upstream via this SOCKS5. Leave blank for direct.
Curated bundle covers vercel, fastly (reddit/cnn/python), AWS CloudFront (netlify), and direct GitHub paths. Edit IPs in config.json if a group stops working.
Loaded curated groups: %1$d added, %2$d already present.
Could not read curated fronting groups asset.
This includes your auth_key and Drive folder/relay settings. It does not include your Drive refresh token or OAuth client secret. Only share with people you trust.
This will replace your current settings.
Camera permission needed to scan QR codes
Enable "Install unknown apps" for rahgozar, then tap the version label to retry.
Downloaded APK size mismatch (%1$d of %2$d bytes).
Estimate — this is what this device relayed. Google\'s dashboard has the authoritative number.
A profile named \'%1$s\' already exists. Tap Overwrite to replace it.
Could not rename — name taken or empty
Switched to \'%1$s\' (live config updated), but saving the active marker failed — restart will lose the marker.
Disconnect first — profile switch only takes effect on next Connect
Could not save config.json — your edit is in-memory only, restart will lose it
profiles.json on disk looks corrupted; refusing to overwrite. Move it aside if you don\'t need it, then retry.
Live config saved, but the profile entry was not saved. Retry to save the profile.
Also works as an upstream proxy for Psiphon / xray — unfronted hosts pass through as raw TCP.
Psiphon needs Android\'s single VPN slot. Stop rahgozar, switch Connection mode to PROXY_ONLY, then Connect again — only then can Psiphon use the address below.
Route specific domains through a CDN edge instead of the Apps Script relay. Type a hostname known to live on the CDN (e.g. python.org → Fastly, react.dev → Vercel) and tap Discover.
domains to front through this edge, one per line
Discovering %1$s — resolving DNS and probing IPs…
Added fronting group for %1$s — fill in domains and Save.
Draft — won\'t save until you list at least one domain.
⚠ Only list domains you know are served by the same edge as the SNI above — a mismatch returns wrong-cert errors or a default page.

1. Paste one or more Apps Script deployment URLs (or bare IDs) and your auth_key.
2. Tap Install MITM certificate. Confirm the dialog — the cert is saved to Downloads/rahgozar-ca.crt and the Settings app opens. Use Settings' search bar to find "CA certificate", tap that result (NOT "VPN & app user certificate" or "Wi-Fi"), and pick rahgozar-ca.crt from Downloads. You'll be asked to set a screen lock if you don't have one (Android requirement).
3. Before tapping Start, expand "SNI pool + tester" and hit "Test all". If every entry times out, your google_ip is unreachable — replace it with one that resolves locally (e.g. `nslookup www.google.com` on any working device).
4. Tap Start. Accept the VPN prompt. The full TUN bridge routes every app on the device through the proxy — no per-app setup needed.
5. If Chrome shows "504 Relay timeout": your Apps Script deployment isn't responding. Redeploy the script, grab the new /exec URL, and paste it above. Watch Live logs for "Relay timeout" vs "connect:" errors to tell which layer is failing.

Known limitation — Cloudflare Turnstile ("Verify you are human") will loop endlessly on most CF-protected sites. Every Apps Script request uses a rotating Google-datacenter egress IP + a fixed "Google-Apps-Script" User-Agent + a Google TLS fingerprint. The cf_clearance cookie is bound to the (IP, UA, JA3) tuple the challenge was solved against, so the NEXT request — from a different egress IP — gets re-challenged. Nothing in this app can fix that; it's inherent to Apps Script as a relay. Sites that only gate the initial page load (not every request) will work after one solve.
